theme: Origami that must fold flat AND thin
title: Pleat-Stack Routing: Minimizing Peak Layer Thickness Under a Maekawa Floor
strategies: trivial: resolve every vertex group's residual parity freedom with a fixed low-index-first Mountain tie-break (this is also the checker's own baseline construction) -> ~0.1

greedy: resolve the same parity floor with a different but equally position-blind tie-break (high-index-first Mountains); a plausible first attempt that fixates on feasibility and never looks at where the routing walk sends the flaps

strong: for each vertex group's residual freedom, simulate every valid Mountain/Valley arrangement's effect on the routing walk and commit to whichever keeps the running peak column weight lowest -- exploiting the fact that Valley choices spread flaps into new columns while Mountain choices reflect the walk back over itself
invalid: assign every free crease Valley without ever checking the vertex-group parity target -> fails the Maekawa-style feasibility floor -> 0
